reviews_data.review_text: Absolutely horrible service. I’ve been going to this Starbucks for months now, pretty much every day of the week unless I have something to do and can’t make it by. I’ve gotten to a point where I’m on name basis with a few of the employees as I see them every morning. I’ve had nothing but great experiences with pretty much every employee there until this morning. Trying to ask a question about the difference in ordering online versus at the intercom because she was scolding me like a child for not giving them enough time to finish the two drinks and one food item, that mind you were done when I got to the window, as they are every single morning. Someone with an attitude problem that severe should not be working window where they are one on one with customers, because obviously she cannot handle it. She couldn’t even handle a simple question without getting rude as well as not even knowing the reason why I couldn’t do what she’s trying to tell me I can’t do. A simple answer where you weren’t being incredibly rude, couldve ended in me learning why I cannot do the thing you’re telling me I can’t do without feeling berated for wanting a $7 drink that I paid for that was handed to me as soon as I got to the window.

reviews_data.review_text:
Business basics, how important is the Customer Experience?
This is one of the reasons I'm rarely a fan of big franchise joints like this. Sometimes, I make an exception. I do like the ability to order from their App and cruise thru the drive-thru and just say 'mobile order for Jon'.

Today when I cruised thru around 930a, I sensed something was up because the line was MUCH longer than usual. This is a very POPULAR location at Baxter Village.

The line was moving SO SLOW!
I saw multiple people parking, going in picking up their order and then back in their vehicle and leaving and I'm STILL sitting in the same spot in the drive-thru line.

Then I saw THE SIGN. The handwritten one scribbled and taped to the order kiosk. Which read 'our speaker is broken'.

By the time you see the sign, there is NO WAY to get out of the line. You are STUCK!

I'm no food service professional but just ask yourself, would it not make more sense to have that sign posted BEFORE you enter the drive-thru line?

Where is the Customer Service?
Where is the Management?
Where is the Leadership?
Where is the individual employee who steps up and says 'HEY, there might be a better way?

No, it won't happen because this is a franchise that is TOO BIG to fail.

I was in line for 30 minutes to pick up a coffee order I had already paid for so I had NO CHOICE but to endure this.

HEY, do you HEAR me Seattle?

reviews_data.review_text: This was the worst experience today at the Kinsley location. I have always had a good experience. I ordered my coffee through the mobile app, in hopes it would be faster. I arrive to the speaker to let them know I have a mobile order, she lets me know they were working on it. Fine, I drive up and another car in front and I wait.  My turn, they tell that mine is not ready so I have to drive around again. What?  I ask what’s the difference if I ordered at the drive thru rather than  mobile order? The girl says, “ I just do what I’m told and we are not rushing mobile orders anymore.” In other words there is no answer other than they are putting priorities on drive thru orders rather than mobile orders. So if you have mobile orders and they aren’t ready be prepared to make circles.

reviews_data.review_text: This Starbucks is awesome! The location and the building design are a pleasant and unique find during our travels. The bathrooms were clean. Probably the most adorable Starbucks I've ever been to. The drive through is a little tight but luckily I went inside.

Everything about the visit was exactly as expected including the super friendly service and quick delivery.

I was perfectly, perfectly fine, until I got an attitude from a barista.

I placed a mobile order for an extremely simple drink. Iced coffee with cream. I got there, I waited until 11, and had to leave. Got a mile up the road and it said order in progress which was fine, I wasn’t far and could drive back when it was ready. Then, my tracking disappeared. So at noon I called, and was offered to come back for them to make it again. I went back, placed it, and watched as dozens of people who ordered after me, got their orders. I was then told it would be ANOTHER 20 minutes for my coffee. When I asked why I’m having to wait for 2 1/2 hours for my drink that was never even made in the first place, the barista started mumbling under her breath and I caught a little bit of what she said which did not sit right with me. Another barista took a venti cup with ice, poured the coffee, the cream, and snapped the lid. All done.

I understand being slammed. I understand being short staffed. I do not understand why I had to wait 2 1/2 hours for a mediocre drink. Never again.

Starbucks remains a beacon of consistency in the world of coffee, with its wide array of beverages and cozy ambiance. The quality and variety of their offerings continue to impress, and the convenience of their locations makes them a go-to spot for many. However, it's hard to overlook the fact that their prices are steadily increasing. While the experience and taste justify the cost for many, some may find themselves hesitant to indulge as frequently due to the higher prices.

I ordered through the mobile app and went inside to pick up my 2 drinks, after I had already left I realized they made my drink wrong so I turned around to get my correct drink and when I arrived I explained to her my drink was peppermint mocha not peppermint white mocha like I had ordered she then proceeded to tell me “it was made how it was ordered” so I pointed out to her that it said WHITE mocha so she said she’d remake it and you could tell she was annoyed she then didn’t read my old ticket and tried to make my drink with 2% so I mentioned to her it was almond milk and she was clearly annoyed and dumped the milk out aggressively. reviews_data.review_text:
Business basics, how important is the Customer Experience?
This is one of the reasons I'm rarely a fan of big franchise joints like this. Sometimes, I make an exception. I do like the ability to order from their App and cruise thru the drive-thru and just say 'mobile order for Jon'.

Today when I cruised thru around 930a, I sensed something was up because the line was MUCH longer than usual. This is a very POPULAR location at Baxter Village.

The line was moving SO SLOW!
I saw multiple people parking, going in picking up their order and then back in their vehicle and leaving and I'm STILL sitting in the same spot in the drive-thru line.

Then I saw THE SIGN. The handwritten one scribbled and taped to the order kiosk. Which read 'our speaker is broken'.
